title: F-A1-PATTERN cross-repo printenv→env_var sweep (Task A follow-up)
scope: Apply the canonshared/sync-readme A.1 fix pattern (env_var(HOME) primary + exec("echo $HOME") fallback) to 12 sites in hexa-lang/example/ + hexa-lang/self/grammar_autofix.hexa that still call exec("printenv HOME") — busybox container without printenv applet returned empty path silently.

parent_witness.raw_47_recommendation: cross-repo trawl-witness — same pattern present in hexa-lang sibling repo

enumeration.discrepancy_note: Brief said 12+1=13; grep finds 12 (11 in example/ + 1 in self/grammar_autofix.hexa). The 13th may have been preemptively counted including the parent A.1 patch already done in canon, OR a stale count from prior enumeration. No additional sites discovered under wider grep ('printenv' with no exec prefix).

sites.context: fn preflight_check: let home = exec("printenv HOME")
fn main: let growth_json = if a.len() >= 4 ... else { exec("printenv HOME") + "/Dev/anima/anima/config/growth_state.json" }
fn find_claude_cli: let home = exec("printenv HOME")
fn config_path: let home_candidates = exec("printenv HOME")

patch_pattern.rationale: Use the proven canonshared/sync-readme.hexa block-statement pattern. Initial attempt with try-as-expression (try { env_var(...) } catch _ { ... }) failed parse — hexa parser rejects try as RHS expression with the catch _ pattern (Parse error at column 77 'unexpected token RBrace'). Block-statement pattern parses cleanly across all 11 files.
patch_pattern.semantics: (1) env_var goes through hexa_env_var → getenv() at C runtime, container-independent. (2) echo "$HOME" fallback uses POSIX shell builtin, available in busybox/dash/bash without external binaries. (3) type_of guard handles env_var returning non-string (nil) gracefully. (4) catch err preserves stack-clean state on hexa runtime exception.

verification.parse_check_all_11_files: PASS (./hexa parse on all 11 modified files produces no Parse error / unexpected token / codegen ERROR; config_test.hexa and growth_tick_test.hexa emit unrelated 'unknown shim command' shell warnings which appear in stderr from a hexa parse hint mechanism but the files themselves parse cleanly — confirmed by 'OK: <file> parses cleanly' line in output)

smoke_test_runtime.result: PASS — printed '=== Pages Deploy Verify ===' header, atlas.n6 path resolved correctly to ~/core/nexus/.. → atlas.n6 v1 10043 nodes detected, GitHub URLs returned 200. End-to-end home resolution works.

raw_18_self_host_fixpoint.verdict: raw 18 self-host fixpoint NEUTRAL — pre-existing infrastructure failure unrelated to this patch. My 12-site sweep does NOT regress self-host (same failure before+after). Independent root-cause investigation deferred — the 'unknown shim command: exe' likely originates from the run-selftest dispatch shim (separate from the `hexa run` binary path), and the self-host fixpoint mechanism here is the legacy --selftest-only path which may have been broken since an unrelated commit. No falsifier trip from this patch.
raw_18_self_host_fixpoint.honest_C3: I did NOT verify cwd-dependent failure of each individual site (mission step 2 was marked optional 'time-permitting'). Verified by transitive evidence: (a) all 12 sites use the identical exec("printenv HOME") pattern that was empirically falsified for the parent A.1 site under the busybox container; (b) the busybox container is shared infrastructure, so the failure mode applies uniformly; (c) the patch is strictly additive (preserves prior behavior under correct getenv, adds shell-builtin fallback). raw 91 honest: per-site cwd reproduction skipped to maintain task velocity.
